# Schplitz

> Privacy-first expense splitting for two people — no server, no account, no cloud.

Schplitz is a client-side web app for splitting shared expenses (drinks, travel, dinners) between two people. All data is encrypted in the browser using AES-256-GCM with a key derived via PBKDF2-SHA256 (200,000 iterations, 128-bit random salt). The encryption key comes from a shared secret answer that never leaves the user's device.

Expenses can be shared with a partner via an encrypted URL. The partner decrypts using the same shared answer. Multi-currency support uses historical exchange rates from the Frankfurter API — the only outbound network call the app ever makes.

## Key facts

- Cipher: AES-256-GCM (Web Crypto API / SubtleCrypto)
- Key derivation: PBKDF2-SHA256, 200,000 iterations
- Salt: 128-bit, randomly generated per session
- IV: 96-bit, randomly generated per ciphertext
- Storage: localStorage (client-side only); encryption key in sessionStorage only
- Server: none
- Database: none
- Account: none required
- Telemetry: none
- Network: optional currency conversion via api.frankfurter.app

## How it works

1. Two people agree on a security question and a shared answer.
2. Each person logs their expenses in the app. Nothing is sent anywhere.
3. One person generates an encrypted share link. The expense data is compressed and encrypted before it enters the URL.
4. The other person opens the link, enters the same answer, and the app decrypts and calculates who owes what — including multi-currency balances at historical rates.

## App

- URL: https://schplitz.com/
- Source: https://github.com/thijsschippers/schplitz
